stories.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
stories.body: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

stories.title: Teenager accused of carrying out sabotage actions in Poland for Russia
stories.body: Polish security services say the aim was to incite ethnic tensions between Poland and Ukraine.

stories.title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
stories.body: Explosions were heard across Iran overnight, shortly after neighbouring Gulf states began to report attacks.

stories.title: Fifa 'assessing match reports' over Falklands banner
stories.body: Fifa is "assessing the match reports" before deciding whether to punish Argentina after players held a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

stories.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
stories.body: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

stories.title: White House teleprompter operator accused of making $100k off Trump speech bets
stories.body: A White House staffer has been accused of using inside knowledge of speeches to make nearly $100,000 on Kalshi.

stories.title: Hundreds of Canada wildfires prompt US air quality alerts as smoke spreads south
stories.body: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has affected cities from Toronto to New York, as well as the US Midwest.

stories.title: Kris Jenner's mother Mary Jo dies aged 91
stories.body: Mary Jo 'MJ' Campbell was featured on multiple episodes of Keeping Up with the Kardashians over the years.

stories.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
stories.body: The same area devastated by floods last year - causing more than 130 deaths, including those at Camp Mystic - is once again being hit.

stories.title: 'Moment of joy' as Uganda discharges last Ebola patient
stories.body: The good news from Uganda comes as the WHO chief warns the virus in DR Congo is spreading fast.

stories.title: Volunteer firefighter suspected of starting devastating France forest fire
stories.body: President Macron said there would be no leniency for arsonists, as France - along with much of Europe - deals with a series of unusual heatwaves.

stories.title: Children among dead at Algeria foster care home blaze - president
stories.body: Eleven people have died and 19 other have suffered injuries in the overnight fire, the authorities say.

stories.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
stories.body: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

stories.title: Fraud crackdown on Ghana ID cards makes digital checks compulsory - photocopies banned
stories.body: Companies are now required to verify a person's identity electronically to curb theft and forgery.

stories.title: JD Vance says Welsh castle was 'coolest thing I've ever seen'
stories.body: The US Vice-President writes in his new book that a visit to north Wales in 2013 changed his perspective.

stories.title: Chip giant TSMC pledges another $100bn to expand US production
stories.body: The company says it will create "high-tech, high-paying jobs", and raises its total commitment to the US to $265bn.

stories.title: Actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, agent confirms
stories.body: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.

stories.title: US military to start screening for testosterone deficiency, Hegseth says
stories.body: Hegseth says he is authorising hormone screening to ensure US troops "operate at your absolute best".

stories.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
stories.body: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

stories.title: Pakistan resisting UK attempts to deport grooming gang leader
stories.body: The government of Pakistan says it has "no connection whatsoever" with the case of Shabir Ahmed.

narrative:
🟡 **Mood: Cautious** — Today's signals reflect a mix of progress and unresolved tensions, with structural patterns leaning toward cautious optimism amid persistent challenges.

The most structurally significant development is the sentencing of Italian officials for the Genoa bridge disaster, marking a rare instance of accountability in a high-profile infrastructure failure. **This signal is convergent, with strong agreement across care-oriented and survival-focused clusters**, underscoring the importance of governance and justice in rebuilding public trust after catastrophic events. The direction is positive, but the contestedness suggests lingering questions about whether this accountability will translate into systemic reforms. This case highlights the broader trajectory of infrastructure safety and governance as critical global priorities.

Other signals, while classified as background noise, carry structural implications worth noting. TSMC's $100 billion pledge to expand U.S. chip production is emblematic of the ongoing reshoring trend in critical industries, driven by geopolitical and supply chain pressures. **Achievement-oriented and stability-seeking clusters dominate this signal**, reflecting the strategic importance of semiconductor independence. Similarly, Ghana's crackdown on ID fraud and Uganda's discharge of its last Ebola patient point to incremental governance improvements and public health victories. While these signals are less contested, their positive direction aligns with broader efforts to enhance institutional resilience.

Contested signals today include Zelensky's removal of Ukraine's defense minister, which has sparked protests and raised concerns about political stability in a wartime context. **This signal is speculative, as the long-term impact of the decision remains unclear**, but it underscores the fragility of governance under duress. Additionally, Iran's targeting of U.S. military bases amid reciprocal strikes reflects escalating tensions in the Middle East, a region where structural volatility persists. These signals warrant close monitoring as they could shift trajectories in governance and conflict dynamics.

Longer-term, today's signals reinforce themes of resilience and accountability. Infrastructure safety, industrial reshoring, and public health improvements are converging as critical areas of focus, while geopolitical flashpoints and contested governance remain key risks. The cautious mood reflects the balance between incremental progress and unresolved systemic challenges.

**Watch for next:** Follow-up on Italy's infrastructure reforms post-sentencing, the operational impact of TSMC's U.S. expansion, and any ripple effects from Zelensky's decision in Ukraine. Additionally, monitor developments in U.S.-Iran tensions and their implications for regional stability.
